description: Learned that ntl-workflow-guidance MUST be queried BEFORE any execution, even when task seems straightforward
root_cause: NTL_Engineer assumed direct execution was acceptable for event-impact analysis without checking for pre-defined workflows
lesson_learned: Always query ntl-workflow-guidance first. Even seemingly simple tasks may have optimized pre-defined workflows (like Q20 for earthquake impact)
corrective_action_taken: Re-executed with full Q20 compliance: multi-scale buffers (25/50/100km), proper date windows, CSV+JSON outputs
prevention_mechanism: Added pre-execution checklist to NTL_Engineer mental model: [ ] Router queried? [ ] Matching workflow found? [ ] Task level confirmed?
impact: Analysis quality improved from incomplete (50km only) to comprehensive (multi-scale 25/50/100km with proper severity assessment)

description: Q20 earthquake impact assessment workflow completed with comprehensive damage report including blackout metrics, comparative analysis, and recommendations
lesson_learned: Full Q20 workflow execution produces high-quality, actionable damage assessment reports with multi-scale analysis
corrective_action_taken: Generated comprehensive Impact_Assessment_Report.json with all required sections: event metadata, ANTL results, blackout metrics, comparative analysis, damage assessment summary, and methodology notes
prevention_mechanism: Maintain Q20 workflow compliance checklist for all future earthquake impact assessments
impact: Delivered actionable damage assessment with severity classification (SEVERE), priority zones identified (50km critical), and recovery recommendations

description: Q20 earthquake impact assessment extended with 4-panel dashboard visualization and comparison bar chart. Band name correction noted for future reference.
lesson_learned: Q20 workflow + visualization is a repeatable pattern for future earthquake impact assessments. Band name DNB_BRDF_Corrected_NTL (not BRDF-Corrected_Radiance) should be used in script contracts.
corrective_action_taken: Generated 4-panel impact dashboard (ANTL bars, drop %, recovery trajectory, recovery ratio) + grouped bar comparison chart. All charts use dark theme (#1a1a2e) with value labels.
prevention_mechanism: Document correct VNP46A2 band name in Q20 workflow notes. Include visualization as standard extension for impact assessments.
impact: Enhanced deliverable with professional 4-panel dashboard showing SEVERE (-42.1%) 25km impact, MILD outer zone impacts, and divergent recovery patterns across buffer scales.

description: Discovered systematic relationship between NTL indicator type and optimal socioeconomic proxy: NTL均值(mean) strongly correlates with population density (ρ=0.9630) but weakly with GDP总量 (ρ=0.2758); TNTL(sum) strongly correlates with GDP总量 (ρ=0.8188). This validates the fundamental distinction between intensity metrics (mean, per-unit-area) and aggregate metrics (sum, total) in NTL-based socioeconomic estimation.
lesson_learned: NTL indicator selection rule: (a) Use NTL均值 (mean) when estimating density-type variables (population density, urbanization intensity, electrification rate); (b) Use TNTL (sum) when estimating aggregate-type variables (GDP总量, total population, total energy consumption). Using the wrong indicator type leads to poor correlation (e.g., NTL均值 vs GDP: ρ=0.28) even when the correct indicator works well (TNTL vs GDP: ρ=0.82).
corrective_action_taken: Established 3-dimensional comparison framework: (1) NTL均值 vs population density ρ=0.9630; (2) NTL均值 vs GDP ρ=0.2758; (3) TNTL vs GDP ρ=0.8188. All validated on China 34 provinces, 2020.
prevention_mechanism: When designing NTL-based socioeconomic estimation workflows, always match indicator type to target variable type: mean→density, sum→aggregate. This should be documented as a mandatory decision gate in indicator estimation workflows.
impact: Provides methodological foundation for GDP estimation (use TNTL, not ANTL), population density estimation (use NTL均值), and general NTL indicator selection for any socioeconomic variable.

description: Country-scale 34-province NTL statistics via GEE reduceRegions. Required 3 iterations: (v1) NTL annual image null -> monthly composite fallback; (v2) maxPixels invalid for reduceRegions -> removed; (v3) Taiwan missing from geoBoundaries CHN -> added explicit polygon.
root_cause: Three systemic issues: (1) NOAA/VIIRS/DNB/ANNUAL_V22/2020 not accessible as direct ee.Image asset; (2) ee.Image.reduceRegions does not accept maxPixels/tileScale (those are reduceRegion-only params); (3) geoBoundaries ADM1 shapeGroup=CHN excludes Taiwan province.
lesson_learned: For country-scale GEE reduceRegions tasks: (a) Use monthly VIIRS composite .mean() as primary fallback when annual V22 images are inaccessible; (b) Never include maxPixels/tileScale in reduceRegions calls; (c) Always add Taiwan as explicit ee.Feature when using geoBoundaries CHN filter.
workflow_updated: statistical_analysis.json (new country_scale_province_ntl_statistics entry added to metrics)
corrective_action_taken: v2: replaced annual image with 12-month monthly composite mean; removed maxPixels/tileScale from reduceRegions. v3: added Taiwan polygon (120-122E, 22-25.5N) as explicit ee.Feature appended to province collection.
prevention_mechanism: Added 4 key lessons to workflow metrics for future country-scale tasks. Taiwan inclusion is now a mandatory checklist item for China provincial statistics.
impact: Delivered complete 34-province ranked NTL table for China 2020, including Taiwan at rank 9 (2.41).

description: Re-ran China 34-province NTL mean vs census density scatter plot workflow. Log-space Pearson r=0.9735, confirming previous Spearman rho=0.9630 result. Reference script china_province_annual_reduceRegions.py template validated.
lesson_learned: The scatter plot workflow (NTL mean + official census density + log-log visualization) is a stable, reproducible pattern. The GEE reduceRegions reference script and census density scatter plot are a reliable template for future province-scale NTL vs socioeconomic comparisons.
corrective_action_taken: Confirmed metrics.json last_updated to 2026-04-28. No new workflow mutation needed — this run validates existing methodology records.
prevention_mechanism: None needed — existing records already capture the methodology. This run serves as confirmation, not discovery.
impact: Validates robustness of NTL mean vs population density correlation (r=0.9735 log-space) as a repeatable analysis pattern.
